name: Chaotic Aether
manualNotes: Analyzer roles accepted during batch clean pass.
oracleText: When you encounter Chaotic Aether, each blank roll of the planar die is a {CHAOS} roll until a player planeswalks away from a plane. (Then planeswalk away from this phenomenon.)

name: Interplanar Tunnel
manualNotes: Excluded from deck role audit: plane/phenomenon card, not a deck slot.
oracleText: When you encounter Interplanar Tunnel, reveal cards from the top of your planar deck until you reveal five plane cards. Put a plane card from among them on top of your planar deck, then put the rest of the revealed cards on the bottom in a random order. (Then planeswalk away from this phenomenon.)

name: Morphic Tide
manualNotes: Excluded from deck role audit: plane/phenomenon card, not a deck slot.
oracleText: When you encounter Morphic Tide, each player shuffles all permanents they own into their library, then reveals that many cards from the top of their library. Each player puts all artifact, creature, land, and planeswalker cards revealed this way onto the battlefield, then does the same for enchantment cards, then puts all cards revealed this way that weren't put onto the battlefield on the bottom of their library in any order. (Then planeswalk away from this phenomenon.)

name: Mutual Epiphany
manualNotes: Analyzer roles accepted during batch clean pass.

name: Planewide Disaster

name: Reality Shaping

name: Spatial Merging

name: Time Distortion
manualNotes: Excluded from deck role audit: plane/phenomenon card, not a deck slot.
oracleText: When you encounter Time Distortion, reverse the game's turn order. (For example, if play had proceeded clockwise around the table, it now goes counterclockwise. Then planeswalk away from this phenomenon.)

name: Akoum

When Aretopolis has ten or more scroll counters on it, planeswalk. Whenever chaos ensues, put a scroll counter on Aretopolis, then draw cards equal to the number of scroll counters on it.

name: Talon Gates
manualNotes: Analyzer roles accepted during batch clean pass.
